volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 4.4.714b - Section 4.6.766c)

sections.label: Section 4.4.714b
sections.translation: Indeed , I do entreat you , Phaedria .

sections.label: Section 4.4.714c
sections.translation: Be off in-doors .

sections.label: Section 4.4.714d
sections.translation: Oh ! oh !

sections.label: Section 4.4.715
sections.original: Alio pacto honeste quo modo hinc abeam nescio .
sections.translation: How in any other fashion to get decently out of this I don ’ t know ; for really it ’ s all up with me . ( Aloud , with pretended indignation .) Will you be trifling with me even here , you knave ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.717
sections.original: Parmenonis tam scio esse hanc technam quam me vivere .
sections.translation: I ’ m as certain that this is the contrivance of Parmeno as that I ’ m alive .

sections.label: Section 4.4.718
sections.translation: So it is , no doubt .

sections.label: Section 4.4.718b
sections.original: Inveniam pol hodie parem ubi referam gratiam .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ ll find out a method to-day to be even with him . But now , what do you think ought to be done , Dorias ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.719b
sections.translation: Do you mean with regard to this

sections.label: Section 4.4.720
sections.translation: girl ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.720b
sections.original: Ita : utrum taceamne an praedicem ? Dor . Tu pol , si sapis ,
sections.translation: Yes ; whether I ought to mention it or be silent ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.721
sections.original: Quod scis nescis , neque de eunucho , neque de vitio virginis :
sections.translation: Upon my word , if you are prudent , [*] you won ’ t know what you do know , either about the Eunuch or the girl ’ s misfortune . By this method you ’ ll both rid yourself of all perplexity , and have done a service to her . [*] Say this only , that Dorus has run away .

sections.label: Section 4.4.723b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.723c
sections.translation: But don ’ t I see Chremes ? Thais will be here just now .

sections.label: Section 4.4.724b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.724c
sections.original: Quia cum inde abeo , iam tum inceperat
sections.translation: Because when I came away from there , a quarrel had just commenced between them .

sections.label: Section 4.4.725b
sections.original: Aufer aurum hoc : ego scibo ex hoc quid siet .
sections.translation: Take in these golden trinkets ; I shall learn from him what ’ s the matter .

sections.label: Section 4.5.726
sections.original: At at ! data hercle verba mihi sunt : vicit vinum quod bibi .
sections.translation: Heyday ! upon my faith , I ’ ve been bamboozled : the wine that I ’ ve drunk has got the upper hand . But , so long as I was reclining , how extremely sober I did seem to myself to be ; when I got up , neither feet nor senses were quite equal to their duty .

sections.label: Section 4.5.729
sections.translation: Chremes !

sections.label: Section 4.5.729b
sections.original: Quis est ? ehem Pythias . Vah , quanto nunc formosior
sections.translation: Who ’ s that ? What , Pythias ; dear me , how much more charming you now

sections.label: Section 4.5.730
sections.translation: seem to me than a short time since !

sections.label: Section 4.5.730b
sections.original: Certe tu quidem pol et multo hilarior .
sections.translation: Troth now , you are much more merry , that ’ s certain .

sections.label: Section 4.5.731
sections.original: Verbum hercle hoc verum est ; sine Cerere et Libero friget Venus .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , it is a true saying , that Venus grows cold without Ceres and Bacchus . But has Thais got here long before me ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.732b
sections.translation: Has she already come away from the Captain ’ s ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.733
sections.original: Iamdudum ; aetatem : lites sunt inter eos factae maximae .
sections.translation: A long time ago ; an age since . There has been a most violent quarrel between them .

sections.label: Section 4.5.734
sections.translation: Did she say nothing about you following her ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.734b
sections.translation: Nothing at all ; only , on going away , she gave me a nod .

sections.label: Section 4.5.735
sections.translation: Well now , wasn ’ t that enough ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.735b
sections.original: At nescibam id dicere illam ; nisi quia
sections.translation: Why , I didn ’ t know that she meant that , until the Captain gave me an explanation , because I was dull of comprehension ; for he bundled me out of the house . But look , here she is ; I wonder how it was I got here before her .

sections.label: Section 4.6.738
sections.original: Credo equidem illum iam adfuturum esse , ut illam a me eripiat . Sine veniat .
sections.translation: I really do believe that he ’ ll be here presently , to force her away from me . Let him come ; but if he touches her with a single finger , that instant his eyes shall be torn out .

sections.label: Section 4.6.740
sections.original: Usque adeo ego illius ferre possum ineptias et magnifica verba ,
sections.translation: I can put up with his impertinences and his high-sounding words , as long as they remain words : but if they are turned into realities , he shall get a drubbing .

sections.label: Section 4.6.742
sections.original: Thais , ego iam dudum hic adsum .
sections.translation: Thais , I ’ ve been here some time .

sections.label: Section 4.6.742b
sections.original: O mi Chreme , te ipsum exspectabam .
sections.translation: O my dear Chremes , you are the very person I was wanting . Are you aware that this quarrel took place on your account , and that the whole of this affair , in fact , bore reference to yourself ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.744b
sections.translation: To me ? How so , pray ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.744c
sections.translation: Because , while I ’ ve been doing my best

sections.label: Section 4.6.745
sections.original: Reddere et restituere , haec atque huiusmodi sum multa passa .
sections.translation: to recover and restore your sister to you , this and a great deal more like it I ’ ve had to put up with .

sections.label: Section 4.6.746
sections.translation: Where is she ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.746b
sections.translation: At home , at my house .

sections.label: Section 4.6.746c
sections.translation: Hah !

sections.label: Section 4.6.747
sections.original: Educta ita uti teque illaque dignum est .
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ? [*] She has been brought up in a manner worthy of yourself and of her .

sections.label: Section 4.6.747b
sections.translation: What is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.747c
sections.translation: That which is the fact . Her I present to you , nor do I ask of you any return for her .

sections.label: Section 4.6.749
sections.original: Et habetur et referetur , Thais , a me ita uti merita es gratia .
sections.translation: Thanks are both felt and shall be returned in such way , Thais , as you deserve .

sections.label: Section 4.6.750
sections.original: At enim cave ne prius quam hanc a me accipias , amittas Chreme ;
sections.translation: But still , take care , Chremes , that you don ’ t lose her , before you receive her from me ; for it is she , whom the Captain is now coming to take away from me by force . Do you go , Pythias , and bring out of the house the casket with the tokens . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.6.753
sections.translation: Don ’ t you see him , Thais ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.753b
sections.translation: Where is it put ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.753c
sections.translation: In the clothes ’ chest . Tiresome creature , why do you delay ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.754
sections.original: Militem secum ad te quantas copias adducere ?
sections.translation: What a large body of troops the Captain is bringing with him against you .

sections.label: Section 4.6.755
sections.translation: Bless me !

sections.label: Section 4.6.755b
sections.original: Num formidolosus obsecro es , mi homo ?
sections.translation: Prithee , are you frightened , my dear sir ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.755c
sections.translation: Get out with you . What , I frightened ? There ’ s not a man alive less so .

sections.label: Section 4.6.757
sections.translation: Then now is the time to prove it .

sections.label: Section 4.6.757b
sections.original: Ah , metuo qualem tu me esse hominem existimes .
sections.translation: Why , I wonder what sort of a man you take me to be .

sections.label: Section 4.6.758
sections.original: Immo hoc cogitato ; quicum res tibi est peregrinus est ,
sections.translation: Nay , and consider this too ; the person that you have to deal with is a foreigner ; [*] of less influence than you , less known , and one that has fewer friends here .

sections.label: Section 4.6.760
sections.original: Scio istuc . Sed tu quod cavere possis stultum admittere est .
sections.translation: I ’ m aware of that ; but it ’ s foolish to run the risk of what you are able to avoid . I had rather we should prevent it , than , having received an injury , avenge ourselves upon him . Do you go in and fasten the door , while I run across hence to the Forum ; I should like us to have the aid of some legal adviser in this disturbance .

sections.label: Section 4.6.763b
sections.translation: Stay .

sections.label: Section 4.6.764
sections.translation: Let me go , I ’ ll be here presently .

sections.label: Section 4.6.764b
sections.translation: There ’ s no occasion , Chremes .

sections.label: Section 4.6.765
sections.original: Hoc modo die , sororem illam tuam esse , et te parvam virginem
sections.translation: Only say that she is your sister , and that you lost her when a little girl , and have now recognized her ; then show the tokens .

sections.label: Section 4.6.766b
sections.translation: Here they are .

sections.label: Section 4.6.766c
sections.translation: Take them . If he offers any violence , summon the fellow to justice ; do you understand me ?
